cardano/address/Credential.title: Credential
cardano/address/Credential.description:
A general structure for representing an on-chain `Credential`.

 Credentials are always one of two kinds: a direct public/private key
 pair, or a script (native or Plutus).

cardano/transaction/OutputReference.title: OutputReference
cardano/transaction/OutputReference.description:
An `OutputReference` is a unique reference to an output on-chain. The `output_index`
 corresponds to the position in the output list of the transaction (identified by its id)
 that produced that output

dcu/group/GroupCip68Datum.title: GroupCip68Datum
dcu/group/GroupCip68Datum.description:
CIP-68 wrapper for the group (100) reference token datum.
 Wallets resolve the group name by reading metadata["name"] from this datum.
 `extra` holds all protocol state; metadata and version are preserved unchanged
 through every state transition except UpdateGroup (which may update them when
 member_count == 0).

dcu/group/GroupDatum.title: GroupDatum
dcu/group/GroupDatum.description: Group Validator Datum — protocol state only, no display metadata.

dcu/group/PayoutMode.title: PayoutMode
dcu/group/PayoutMode.description:
Payout delivery mode, fixed at group creation and frozen once members join.
   Push = current behaviour: DistributeRound builds a wallet output paying the
          borrower the pro-rata pot directly. Backwards compatible default.
   Pull  = DistributeRound earmarks the pot into the borrower's OWN treasury UTxO
          (TreasuryState.claimable_balance); the borrower later withdraws it to any
          address via ClaimPayout (gated by possession of the member 222 token).
          Solves the lost-wallet problem; entitlement is durable until claimed or exit.

dcu/treasury/TreasuryRedeemer.title: TreasuryRedeemer
dcu/treasury/TreasuryRedeemer.description:
Treasury Validator Redeemer.

 The treasury script is a thin DISPATCHER: all heavy validation lives in the four
 family stake validators (rounds / lifecycle / recovery / reserve — hashes published
 in ProtocolSettings) and runs ONCE per tx in their `withdraw` handlers. The action
 indices live on the family withdraw redeemer (single source of truth), located by
 PURPOSE (`Withdraw(Script(family_hash))` in tx.redeemers) — so the spend/mint
 variants here carry no fields. The dispatcher requires the family action to COVER
 the spent UTxO (the pin rule, see dcu/dispatch). DistributeRound keeps its original
 shape: the rounds indexer enforces completeness globally, so a withdrawal-presence
 check (`withdrawal_index` into tx.withdrawals) suffices.

 Variant ORDER is unchanged from the monolith (constr indices stable; the field
 shapes are the wire-level break of this cycle).

dcu/treasury/TreasuryWithdrawRedeemer.title: TreasuryWithdrawRedeemer
dcu/treasury/TreasuryWithdrawRedeemer.description:
Treasury Withdraw-Validator Redeemer (the "withdraw-zero" coupling).

 DistributeRound spends N member treasury UTxOs in one tx. Running the global member-set
 validation in the per-input `spend` handler re-executes it N times → O(N³). Instead, that
 heavy logic runs ONCE here in the
 treasury `withdraw` handler (triggered by a 0-ADA reward withdrawal from the
 treasury's own stake credential), and each `spend` only asserts this withdrawal is
 present. The treasury validator is self-coupled: the staking script hash == the
 spending script hash == the treasury policy id.

dcu/treasury_actions/LifecycleAction.title: LifecycleAction
dcu/treasury_actions/LifecycleAction.description: Lifecycle family — membership and round-cycle operations.

dcu/treasury_actions/RecoveryAction.title: RecoveryAction
dcu/treasury_actions/RecoveryAction.description: Recovery family — lost-member identity rotation (quorum + timelock + veto).

dcu/treasury_actions/ReserveAction.title: ReserveAction
dcu/treasury_actions/ReserveAction.description: Reserve family — the group's mutual reserve pot.
